Geological Eras.description: Formation of the Earth's crust, primitive oceans and intense bombardment.
First life forms (prokaryotes) and first continents.
Oxygenation of the atmosphere and appearance of complex cells.

Tectonics & Geology.description: Accretion of matter forming the primitive Earth.
Impact with a protoplanet (Theia) forming the Moon.
Major increase of oxygen in the atmosphere.
Global glaciations covering almost the entire planet.
All continents united into a single supercontinent.
Formation of the Alps and the Himalayas.

Evolution of Life.description: Bacteria, archaea — first life forms (oceans).
Cells with a nucleus; then multicellularity.
Algae, then animals.
Plants, arthropods and vertebrates colonize the continents.
Domination of dinosaurs over the continents.
Diversification after the extinction of the dinosaurs.
Appearance of humans and evolution of the genus Homo.

Geology & Tectonics.description: Birth of the Sun and the planets around 4.6 billion years ago.
Accretion of the planet Earth from the protoplanetary disk.
Likely the result of a collision with a Mars-sized body (Theia).

Geology & Tectonics.titre: Formation of Oceans and First Continental Crust
Geology & Tectonics.description: Accumulation of liquid water on the surface and beginnings of primitive continents.
Intense meteorite rain on the primitive Earth and Moon.
First significant accumulation of oxygen in the atmosphere.
Glaciers extending over a large part of the planet (Snowball Earth).
Single supercontinent uniting almost all of the landmasses.

Geology & Tectonics.titre: Formation of the Appalachians and Other Major Ranges

Geology & Tectonics.description: Beginning of continental drift toward their current positions (Laurasia and Gondwana).

Evolution of Life.description: First unicellular life forms in the oceans.
First visible fossil traces of life, produced by cyanobacteria.
Cells with a nucleus appear.
First multicellular organisms (algae, then animals).
First complex multicellular animal organisms, before the Cambrian explosion.
Rapid diversification of complex marine life.
Colonization of the continents by vegetation (mosses, then vascularization).
First jawed vertebrates with teeth (gnathostomes).
Colonization of land by arthropods.
Four-limbed vertebrates out of water, aquatic-terrestrial transition.
First insects capable of flight, during the Carboniferous.
Complete adaptation to life on land.
First dinosaurs (Triassic); diversification in the Jurassic and Cretaceous.
Ancestors of modern crocodiles, appeared in the Triassic.
Appearance in the Triassic (small, nocturnal).
Archaeopteryx, evolutionary link between dinosaurs and modern birds.
Radiation of angiosperms, transforming terrestrial ecosystems.
Rapid evolutionary radiation after the disappearance of the dinosaurs.
Ancestors of monkeys and hominids.

Extinctions.description: Greatest mass extinction in history, disappearance of ~90% of marine species.
Disappearance of the dinosaurs (Chicxulub meteorite); niches freed for mammals.

Hominids & Prehistory.description: Last common ancestor between the human lineage and that of chimpanzees.
First members of the genus Homo in Africa, tools, advanced bipedalism.
First anatomically modern humans, in Africa.
Migration of Homo sapiens out of Africa and gradual peopling of the world.
Domestication of plants and animals, settlement of populations.
End of the last glaciation, current epoch.
Disappearance of large mammals such as the mammoth.
